skill.name: react-best-practices
skill.description: React and Next.js performance guidance for agents that write, review, or refactor application code.
skill.summary: Apply practical React and Next.js performance rules during code generation and review.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The large static hit count is mostly false positives from markdown code examples, references, and generated documentation. No prompt injection, hidden executable payload, or malicious network intent was found. Medium warnings remain because the guidance includes inline script and cookie logging examples that can create security risk if copied without review.

medium_findings.title: Security-Sensitive Inline Script Recommendation
medium_findings.description: The guide recommends using dangerouslySetInnerHTML with an inline script that reads localStorage before hydration. This can be valid for flicker prevention, but it weakens CSP posture and can normalize risky inline scripting if copied broadly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly show dangerouslySetInnerHTML with inline JavaScript. The intent is performance guidance, so the concern is misuse rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Cookie Value in Logging Example
medium_findings.description: A server-side example reads a session cookie value and passes it to a logging function after the response. Logging session identifiers can expose credentials or tracking identifiers in application logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example directly obtains a session-id cookie and sends it to logUserAction. The function is illustrative, but the pattern is security-sensitive in real applications.

low_findings.title: Static Code Execution Flags Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Dynamic imports, shell commands, and command-like backticks appear inside markdown guidance and README instructions. No executable package manifest or source script was present in the scanned skill directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns are inside fenced examples or markdown text. I found no package.json or src directory in the skill directory to execute these snippets.

low_findings.title: Network Indicators Are References or Local Examples
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs are project references, and fetch examples use local API paths in documentation. No evidence found of unauthorized outbound network calls by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are documentation references, and the fetch example targets a relative application API. There is no runtime code that sends data externally.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Flags Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are triggered by words such as impact labels, markdown filenames, browser API examples, and performance terminology. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation or host reconnaissance intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged locations contain prose and browser performance examples, not encryption code or environment discovery. The scanner appears to be matching substrings without semantic context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Inline JavaScript Through dangerouslySetInnerHTML
dangerous_patterns.description: The example uses an inline script for pre-hydration DOM updates. This should be treated as a security-sensitive pattern and guarded by CSP, nonce strategy, and strict control of interpolated values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pattern is explicit and concrete. It is documentation rather than active code, which lowers severity but not the need for warning.

dangerous_patterns.title: Session Cookie Included in Logging Flow
dangerous_patterns.description: The logging example passes a session cookie value into an audit or analytics helper. Real implementations should avoid logging raw session identifiers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code example directly references session-id and logUserAction. It may be illustrative, but it is a recognizable sensitive-data logging pattern.

content.value_statement: React and Next.js projects often suffer from waterfalls, oversized bundles, and avoidable re-renders. This skill gives agents concrete rules for faster data fetching, smaller client bundles, and more efficient rendering.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to identify and remove sequential async waterfalls.
Recommends bundle-size improvements such as direct imports and dynamic loading.
Explains server-side React and Next.js data fetching patterns.
Covers client data deduplication with SWR and shared subscriptions.
Provides re-render, rendering, and JavaScript hot-path optimization rules.
Uses before-and-after examples for many React and Next.js patterns.
limitations: It is guidance, not an automated profiler or benchmark runner.
Some recommendations require project-specific validation before adoption.
It focuses on performance and does not replace a security review.
Examples may need updates for framework versions and local conventions.

use_cases.title: Review a React Pull Request
use_cases.description: Find waterfalls, excess re-renders, and bundle risks before merging UI changes.

use_cases.title: Refactor a Next.js Route
use_cases.description: Restructure server components, route handlers, and Suspense boundaries for faster responses.

use_cases.title: Guide Agent Code Generation
use_cases.description: Give Claude, Codex, or Claude Code specific performance rules while generating application code.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component for simple performance issues using the React best practices skill. Focus on obvious waterfalls, re-renders, and bundle risks.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Data Fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Next.js route or server component to reduce async waterfalls. Explain which requests can run in parallel and why.

prompt_templates.title: Reduce Client Bundle Size
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these imports and client components for bundle-size problems. Recommend direct imports, dynamic imports, or deferred loading where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Perform Full Optimization Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the full React best practices skill to review this feature. Prioritize critical findings, include tradeoffs, and separate performance changes from security-sensitive changes.

output_examples.input: A page awaits user data, configuration, and sidebar data one after another.
output: Flags the sequential awaits as a critical waterfall.
Suggests starting independent requests earlier and awaiting them together.
Explains which data dependency prevents full parallelization.

output_examples.input: A client component imports a heavy editor and icon library on initial load.
output: Identifies the editor as a candidate for dynamic loading.
Recommends direct icon imports or package import optimization.
Notes that user intent can trigger preload before opening the editor.

output_examples.input: A component recalculates derived UI state and recreates callbacks on every render.
output: Recommends functional state updates for stable callbacks.
Separates useful memoization from unnecessary optimization.

best_practices: Apply the highest-impact rules first, especially async parallelization and bundle reduction.
Validate recommendations against the project framework version and deployment model.
Treat inline scripts, cookies, and logging examples as security-sensitive changes.
anti_patterns: Do not apply every micro-optimization before measuring larger performance bottlenecks.
Do not copy examples that touch cookies or inline scripts without security review.
Do not change architecture solely to satisfy a rule when dependencies require sequencing.

faq.question: Does this skill edit code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance that an agent can apply while reviewing, generating, or refactoring code.

faq.question: Is it only for Next.js?
faq.answer: No. Many rules apply to React generally, but several server and routing rules are specific to Next.js.

faq.question: Can it replace performance testing?
faq.answer: No. It helps find likely issues, but teams should still measure production behavior.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: Most static findings are markdown examples, but some guidance includes inline scripts and cookie logging examples.

faq.question: Does it include security best practices?
faq.answer: Only indirectly. It is a performance skill, so security-sensitive changes still need separate review.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
